scenes.text: In the first scene of our JavaScript lesson plan, we will delve into the basics of JavaScript functions. This includes understanding what a function is, how to define it, and the significance of arguments and return values. We will illustrate this with a simple function declaration and usage example, which will aid in grasping the essential syntax and logic. This foundational knowledge is vital for developing functional applications effectively.

In the second scene of the lecture, we'll explore JavaScript flow control structures, which determine the order of execution in a program. We'll focus on conditional statements that allow executing different blocks of code based on certain conditions. This section will provide a clear understanding of how logical operations are interwoven with conditions, helping make the application decision-process dynamic and interactive.
scenes.image_prompt:
flowchart TD
    Start[Start] --> Check{Condition}
    Check -->|true| Execute[Execute Block A]
    Execute --> End1[End]
    Check -->|false| Execute2[Execute Block B]
    Execute2 --> End2[End]
